sections.paragraph:
ἀποφορτίζομαι nautical t.t. (fig. Jos., Bell. 1, 172; 266), mostly of ‘jettisoning’ cargo in a storm (Philo Mech. 2, 143; Athen. 2, 5, 37cf; Philo, Praem. 33; Pollux 1, 99), but also of regular unloading (Dionys. Hal. 3, 44 αἱ μείζους νῆες ἀπογεμίζονται καὶ ἀποφορτίζονται σκάφαις) unload τ. γόμον the cargo Ac 21:3 (Mod. Gk. ἀποφορτώνω).—DELG s.v. φόρτος.

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Philo Philo Mech , III–II b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Mod. Gk. Mod. Gk. = Modern Greek

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 125
